metaTitle: Image Resizer — Resize Photos for Instagram & YouTube (Free)
metaDescription: Resize up to 20 images at once instantly in your browser. Use SNS presets or custom dimensions. 100% private — no upload required. High-quality Pica scaling for JPG, PNG, WebP.
introSummary: Our professional Batch Image Resizer is the ultimate productivity tool for social media managers and designers. Process up to 20 images simultaneously with one click. Whether you need to adapt photos for Instagram (1080x1350), YouTube thumbnails (1280x720), or custom web assets, our tool ensures crisp results using the industry-leading Pica scaling engine. Experience 100% private, on-device processing where your files never leave your computer.

contextUsage.title: Bulk Social Media Prep
contextUsage.desc: Prepare an entire week's worth of content in seconds. Batch resize up to 20 photos to perfectly fit Instagram, TikTok, or X (Twitter) using our smart presets.

contextUsage.title: Web Performance at Scale
contextUsage.desc: Optimize your website's SEO by batch-resizing large galleries to specific UI dimensions, reducing page weight without uploading sensitive assets to a server.

contextUsage.title: Professional Asset Handling
contextUsage.desc: Quickly standardize product photos or headshots for corporate portals. Our 'Contain' mode ensures every image fits the target size while preserving its original aspect ratio.

howto.description: Drag and drop up to 20 JPG, PNG, or WebP files. Our tool uses local browser threads, so even 8K images are handled without using your network bandwidth.
Choose a preset like 'Insta' or enter custom Width/Height. In Batch Mode, images are automatically scaled to fit (Contain) within these dimensions to prevent cropping.
Click 'Resize'. Our engine applies the Lanczos3 filter via Pica, processing multiple images in parallel to ensure maximum speed and professional sharpness.
Preview your results instantly. For single images, save directly; for batches, grab everything in a single, organized ZIP file generated right in your browser memory.

why: Parallel Batch Processing — Resize up to 20 images at once using multi-threaded browser workers for lightning-fast performance.
Smart 'Contain' Scaling — Automatically fits images of different orientations into your target size without distorting or stretching the subject.
Privacy-First (No Upload) — Your work stays private. Processing happens 100% on your device, making it safe for sensitive or proprietary company assets.
Pica Scaling Engine — Uses advanced interpolation algorithms (Lanczos3) that outperform standard browser resizing for superior clarity.
One-Click SNS Presets — Instantly apply standard sizes for Instagram, YouTube, TikTok, LinkedIn, Facebook, and X (Twitter).
ZIP Export — Save time by downloading all processed images in a single ZIP file, maintaining original filenames with new dimension suffixes.

faq.q: Can I resize multiple images with different aspect ratios at once?
faq.a: Yes! In Batch Mode, our tool uses 'Contain' logic. This means each image will scale down to fit within your chosen dimensions while keeping its original shape, so nothing gets stretched.

faq.q: Is there a limit to how many images I can process?
faq.a: You can process up to 20 images per batch. This ensures your browser remains stable and responsive while delivering high-speed results.

faq.q: Why is 'No-Upload' important for image resizing?
faq.a: Most online tools send your photos to a server, which can be a security risk. Our tool works entirely in your browser (serverless), ensuring your data never leaves your device.

faq.q: Will my resized PNG images still have a transparent background?
faq.a: Absolutely. Our engine fully supports alpha channel transparency for PNG and WebP files, making it perfect for logos and UI icons.

faq.q: What happens if an image fails to resize?
faq.a: If a file is corrupted or too large for your browser's memory, the batch list will show a 'Failed' status for that specific item while continuing to process the rest.

technicalSpecs.useCase: Concurrent multi-file processing via Web Workers
Zero data transfer; works offline after initial load
Flexible saving options for bulk or single edits
Handles professional DSLR and mobile high-res photos

troubleshooting: Images appearing with black bars? This is part of 'Contain' mode to prevent stretching. If you need a perfect fill, try resizing one by one or using a crop tool first.
Browser feeling sluggish? Processing 20 large images uses significant RAM. Close other tabs or process in smaller batches if you are on a device with limited memory.
ZIP file not downloading? Ensure your browser allows multiple file downloads or check for a 'Pop-up blocked' notification in your address bar.
proTips: For the best Instagram results, use the 1080x1350 (Portrait) preset. It covers more screen area than standard squares and gets more engagement.
Social Media Managers: Use the Batch feature to resize your entire campaign gallery to 1200x675 for X (Twitter) in one go to maintain a consistent feed look.
Combine with Compression: For ultimate SEO, resize your images here first, then use our 'Image Compressor' to shrink the file size before uploading to your site.
